title: carex ferruginea subsp. austroalpina
intro: Carex ferruginea subsp. austroalpina, also known as the rusty sedge, is a subspecies of the Carex ferruginea plant, a type of sedge found in the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The rusty sedge has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Carex ferruginea subsp. austroalpina can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and roots can also be used in infusions and decoctions for various herbal remedies.
